Did you ever read Euro-GBC resolutions..?

Social · asked by user [] · 2009-03-07 · 3 answers
I mean ...[url=http://www.eurogbc.com/index.php?option=content&task=view&id=151&Itemid=89]for example...[/url
user [2] · 2009-03-07
I do zzzzzz rea d t hm....zzzzzzzzzz
user [17] · 2009-03-07
They are following the footsteps of the Global GBC (notice the new fancy name)

...use a lot "whereas"
...are fond of forming committees to pretend they do something
...are capable of filling a few paper sheets with nothing in particular
...probably nobody but them pay attention to the resolutions
and.... after the meeting all of them are feeling more important than before

What really amuses me is the capacity to think of themselves God'b4s hands and hammer as a group. They clearly realize "myself am not but a mere faulty individual" and probably they think of the other members like that too.... but ohhh magic, a group of fallible men become infallible by dint of a meeting under a name. A-m-a-z-i-n-g.
If not, why are they not listening anyone?
user [19] · 2009-03-08
[quote][cite] ccd:[/cite]I mean ...[url=http://www.eurogbc.com/index.php?option=content&task=view&id=151&Itemid=89]for example...[/url[/quote]
That is the most boring thing i read in ages.
